steps.title: Calculate buildings on farmland as a percentage of the total plot
steps.content:
Farmland comprises 52% of the total plot. Buildings exist on 21.5% of the farmland. Therefore, the area with buildings on farmland is:
$$21.5\% \times 52\% = 0.215 \times 0.52 = 0.1118 = 11.18\%$$
So 11.18% of the total plot has buildings on the farmland.

steps.title: Calculate buildings on pasture as a percentage of the total plot
steps.content:
Pasture comprises the remaining portion: $100\% - 52\% = 48\%$ of the plot. Buildings exist on 14% of the pasture. Therefore:
$$14\% \times 48\% = 0.14 \times 0.48 = 0.0672 = 6.72\%$$
So 6.72% of the total plot has buildings on the pasture.

steps.title: Sum the percentages to find p
steps.content:
The total percentage of the plot with buildings is the sum of buildings on farmland and buildings on pasture:
$$p = 11.18\% + 6.72\% = 17.9\%$$
Therefore, $p = 17.9$.
